visa_types.processing_time:
You need to submit your application in good time before travelling. According to article 9 section 1 in the visa code, EU 2019/1155, you should apply not less than 15 days before the planned visit begins. The 15 days is counted from when the application is received by the Embassy of Sweden in Nairobi. Consequently, you must calculate with the transportation time when planning for your submission.
A late application might be refused or in some cases a request for a new flight booking, insurance and even new invitation will be required. A request for new documents is likely to increase the handling time further.
The normal transportation time, one way is 1 working day for Nairobi, Kenya.
The Embassy should handle your application within 15 days (if the application is admissible), counted from when the Embassy has received the application. If an application is not complete or if an interview or document verification is needed the handling time can be extended up to 45 days.
General inquiries about Schengen visas should be addressed to 254 2051479 08 (tel:254 2051479 08) or info.Seke@vfshelpline.com (mailto:info.Seke@vfshelpline.com) . If you already have applied and have an inquiry please contact the Embassy of Sweden in Nairobi by email (ambassaden.nairobi-visum@gov.se (mailto:ambassaden.nairobi-visum@gov.se)) or phone. The Embassy´s phone hours are Monday and Wednesdays 2-3 pm and Fridays 11am-12noon local Kenyan time. See the Embassy´s Website for holidays and such at https://www.swedenabroad.se/en/embassies/kenya-nairobi/contact/ (https://www.swedenabroad.se/en/embassies/kenya-nairobi/contact/). During the peak season (May-July and November-December) you need to wait patiently for a reply (3-6 days).

visa_types.overview:
Only the following categories of family members can still join the UK national Withdrawal Agreement beneficiary:
- Family members who were directly related to the Withdrawal Agreement beneficiary (‘core family members’) and resided outside the host State on 31 December 2020 and who fulfil the relevant conditions of Article 2(2) of the Free Movement Directive 2004/38/EC at the moment they seek residence under the Withdrawal Agreement in order to join the beneficiary in the host State
- Children born to, or legally adopted by, a UK national Withdrawal Agreement beneficiary
- Those who were durable partners and resided outside the host State before 31 December 2020 and whose relationship with the UK national Withdrawal Agreement beneficiary continues at the time they seek residence in the host State.
- Even though not explicitly mentioned in Article 10 of the EU-UK Withdrawal Agreement, persons other than those defined in Article 3(2) of Directive 2004/38/EC whose presence is required by UK nationals in order not to deprive them of their Withdrawal Agreement right of residence can also still join the UK national in the host Member State.
Issue of entry visas
The EU-UK Withdrawal Agreement does not govern short stays of family members of Withdrawal Agreement beneficiaries in the host State for the purposes of visiting the beneficiaries. Family members of Withdrawal Agreement beneficiaries who want to enter the EU for a short stay have to apply for a visa under the general provisions of the Visa Code and comply with the regular entry conditions for non-EU nationals, which are set out in the Schengen Borders Code.
Only family members who seek residence in the host State under the provisions of the EU-UK Withdrawal Agreement in order to join the Withdrawal Agreement beneficiary there (i.e., the family members want to become EU-UK Withdrawal Agreement beneficiaries themselves) can rely on the visa facilitations of Article 14(3) of the EU-UK Withdrawal Agreement.
Where family members decide not to make use of their right to lodge their application directly at the consulate but to use the services of an external service provider (VFS) or extra services, they may be required to pay for these services (service fee). Family members are exempted from paying the visa fee.  
Book an appointment
As a family member of an EU citizen, you also have the possibility to submit your visa application directly at the Swedish Embassy in Kenya.
Send an email to ambassaden.nairobivisum@gov.se (mailto:ambassaden.nairobivisum@gov.se) in order to receive an appointment. You will usually be given an appointment within a few working days.
More information
The EU-UK Withdrawal Agreement (europa.eu) (https://commission.europa.eu/strategy-and-policy/relations-non-eu-countries/relations-united-kingdom/eu-uk-withdrawal-agreement_en)
Permission for family members of British citizens - Migrationsverket (https://www.migrationsverket.se/English/Private-individuals/British-citizens/Permission-for-family-members-of-British-citizens.html)
Third countries whose nationals or specific categories of such third country nationals who are subject to prior consultation 
Under Article 22 of the Visa Code, a Member State may require the central authorities of other Member States to consult its central authorities during the examination of visa applications lodged by nationals of specific third countries or specific categories of such nationals. Such consultation does not apply to applications for airport transit visas. The table below lists the third countries and the specific categories concerned. When a third country is listed it means that at least one Member State requires such prior consultation. The consultation might take 1-7 days.
Annexure 16 Prior Consultation List (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5cTmlz6WRVuHYfbRUygKDW/260f8052aad704ad0801fe40b53373b3/annex-16-prior-consultation-en.pdf)
visa_types.full_text:
EU-UK Withdrawal Agreement
UK nationals who resided in an EU Member State in accordance with EU law on free movement of persons on 31 December 2020, and who continued to reside there thereafter, fall within scope of the EU-UK Withdrawal Agreement.

## Overview
Only the following categories of family members can still join the UK national Withdrawal Agreement beneficiary:
- Family members who were directly related to the Withdrawal Agreement beneficiary (‘core family members’) and resided outside the host State on 31 December 2020 and who fulfil the relevant conditions of Article 2(2) of the Free Movement Directive 2004/38/EC at the moment they seek residence under the Withdrawal Agreement in order to join the beneficiary in the host State
- Children born to, or legally adopted by, a UK national Withdrawal Agreement beneficiary
- Those who were durable partners and resided outside the host State before 31 December 2020 and whose relationship with the UK national Withdrawal Agreement beneficiary continues at the time they seek residence in the host State.
- Even though not explicitly mentioned in Article 10 of the EU-UK Withdrawal Agreement, persons other than those defined in Article 3(2) of Directive 2004/38/EC whose presence is required by UK nationals in order not to deprive them of their Withdrawal Agreement right of residence can also still join the UK national in the host Member State.
Issue of entry visas
The EU-UK Withdrawal Agreement does not govern short stays of family members of Withdrawal Agreement beneficiaries in the host State for the purposes of visiting the beneficiaries. Family members of Withdrawal Agreement beneficiaries who want to enter the EU for a short stay have to apply for a visa under the general provisions of the Visa Code and comply with the regular entry conditions for non-EU nationals, which are set out in the Schengen Borders Code.
Only family members who seek residence in the host State under the provisions of the EU-UK Withdrawal Agreement in order to join the Withdrawal Agreement beneficiary there (i.e., the family members want to become EU-UK Withdrawal Agreement beneficiaries themselves) can rely on the visa facilitations of Article 14(3) of the EU-UK Withdrawal Agreement.
Where family members decide not to make use of their right to lodge their application directly at the consulate but to use the services of an external service provider (VFS) or extra services, they may be required to pay for these services (service fee). Family members are exempted from paying the visa fee.  
Book an appointment
As a family member of an EU citizen, you also have the possibility to submit your visa application directly at the Swedish Embassy in Kenya.
Send an email to ambassaden.nairobivisum@gov.se (mailto:ambassaden.nairobivisum@gov.se) in order to receive an appointment. You will usually be given an appointment within a few working days.
More information
The EU-UK Withdrawal Agreement (europa.eu) (https://commission.europa.eu/strategy-and-policy/relations-non-eu-countries/relations-united-kingdom/eu-uk-withdrawal-agreement_en)
Permission for family members of British citizens - Migrationsverket (https://www.migrationsverket.se/English/Private-individuals/British-citizens/Permission-for-family-members-of-British-citizens.html)
Third countries whose nationals or specific categories of such third country nationals who are subject to prior consultation 
Under Article 22 of the Visa Code, a Member State may require the central authorities of other Member States to consult its central authorities during the examination of visa applications lodged by nationals of specific third countries or specific categories of such nationals. Such consultation does not apply to applications for airport transit visas. The table below lists the third countries and the specific categories concerned. When a third country is listed it means that at least one Member State requires such prior consultation. The consultation might take 1-7 days.
Annexure 16 Prior Consultation List (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5cTmlz6WRVuHYfbRUygKDW/260f8052aad704ad0801fe40b53373b3/annex-16-prior-consultation-en.pdf)

## Photo Specifications
Please provide one recent photograph (not more than 1 month old) of yourself, which should not have been used previously in the passport. The photograph should be in colour and: 
- Taken against a light background (white or off-white) so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear share quality and with the face in focus.
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print).
- Full face, non-smiling [without sunglasses, a hat/cap or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of their religious belief or ethnic background].
- Please stick the photograph on the Visa Application Form.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete. A photo booth
                                                                        meeting these requirements is available at the centre.
Kindly download the Photo Specification file (Pdf/photo-specification.pdf) for more information

## Processing Time
You need to submit your application in good time before travelling. According to article 9 section 1 in the visa code, EU 2019/1155, you should apply not less than 15 days before the planned visit begins. The 15 days is counted from when the application is received by the Embassy of Sweden in Nairobi. Consequently, you must calculate with the transportation time when planning for your submission.
A late application might be refused or in some cases a request for a new flight booking, insurance and even new invitation will be required. A request for new documents is likely to increase the handling time further.
The normal transportation time, one way is 1 working day for Nairobi, Kenya.
The Embassy should handle your application within 15 days (if the application is admissible), counted from when the Embassy has received the application. If an application is not complete or if an interview or document verification is needed the handling time can be extended up to 45 days.
General inquiries about Schengen visas should be addressed to 254 2051479 08 (tel:254 2051479 08) or info.Seke@vfshelpline.com (mailto:info.Seke@vfshelpline.com) . If you already have applied and have an inquiry please contact the Embassy of Sweden in Nairobi by email (ambassaden.nairobi-visum@gov.se (mailto:ambassaden.nairobi-visum@gov.se)) or phone. The Embassy´s phone hours are Monday and Wednesdays 2-3 pm and Fridays 11am-12noon local Kenyan time. See the Embassy´s Website for holidays and such at https://www.swedenabroad.se/en/embassies/kenya-nairobi/contact/ (https://www.swedenabroad.se/en/embassies/kenya-nairobi/contact/). visa_types.name: Visiting family and friends
visa_types.visa_fees:
New Visa Fees are effective from 13th February 2026

[Table:Visiting family and friends]

Please Note:
The visa fee shall be waived for applicants belonging to one of the following categories:
- Children under six years
- School pupils, students, postgraduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake stays for the purpose of study or educational training
- Researchers travelling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research or participating in a scientific seminar or conference
- Representatives of non-profit organizations aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organized by non-profit organizations
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports when travelling for official duties
- Family members of EU/EEA citizens
- Family members of UK Nationals who fall under the EU-UK Withdrawal Agreement
- The exchange rate from EURO to KES may vary from time to time. The applicants are requested to confirm the fee before applying.
- VFS service fee and Visa fee can be paid via M-pesa at Visa Application Centre after completion of documents checks by the VFS Counter Staff.
- Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost. Please see more detail from Additional Services tab.

This visa is provided for those who wishes to visit relatives and or friends in Sweden. ## Photo Specifications
Please provide one recent photograph (not more than 1 month old) of yourself, which should not have been used previously in the passport. The photograph should be in colour and: 
- Taken against a light background (white or off-white) so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear share quality and with the face in focus.
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print).
- Full face, non-smiling [without sunglasses, a hat/cap or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of their religious belief or ethnic background].
- Please stick the photograph on the Visa Application Form.

                                                                        meeting these requirements is available at the centre.

## Processing Time
You need to submit your application in good time before travelling. According to article 9 section 1 in the visa code, EU 2019/1155, you should apply not less than 15 days before the planned visit begins. The 15 days is counted from when the application is received by the Embassy of Sweden in Nairobi. Consequently, you must calculate with the transportation time when planning for your submission.
A late application might be refused or in some cases a request for a new flight booking, insurance and even new invitation will be required. A request for new documents is likely to increase the handling time further.
The normal transportation time, one way is 1 working day for Nairobi, Kenya.
The Embassy should handle your application within 15 days (if the application is admissible), counted from when the Embassy has received the application. If an application is not complete or if an interview or document verification is needed the handling time can be extended up to 45 days.
General inquiries about Schengen visas should be addressed to 254 2051479 08 (tel:254 2051479 08) or info.Seke@vfshelpline.com (mailto:info.Seke@vfshelpline.com) . If you already have applied and have an inquiry please contact the Embassy of Sweden in Nairobi by email (ambassaden.nairobi-visum@gov.se (mailto:ambassaden.nairobi-visum@gov.se)) or phone. The Embassy´s phone hours are Monday and Wednesdays 2-3 pm and Fridays 11am-12noon local Kenyan time. See the Embassy´s Website for holidays and such at https://www.swedenabroad.se/en/embassies/kenya-nairobi/contact/ (https://www.swedenabroad.se/en/embassies/kenya-nairobi/contact/). visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you wish to travel to Sweden to attend a course, to conduct research or any other study related purpose please see the list of requirements for further details. You must have an invitation
                                                                        or admission letter from a receiving educational/research institution in Sweden.
Third countries whose nationals or specific categories of such third country nationals who are subject to prior consultation 
Under Article 22 of the Visa Code, a Member State may require the central authorities of other Member States to consult its central authorities during the examination of visa applications lodged by nationals of specific third countries or specific categories of such nationals. Such consultation does not apply to applications for airport transit visas. The table below lists the third countries and the specific categories concerned. When a third country is listed it means that at least one Member State requires such prior consultation. The consultation might take 1-7 days.
Annexure 16 Prior Consultation List (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5cTmlz6WRVuHYfbRUygKDW/260f8052aad704ad0801fe40b53373b3/annex-16-prior-consultation-en.pdf)

Overview
Third-country nationals who are family members of EU citizens derive their rights under the Directive (2004/38/EC) from EU citizens, the holders of the primary status. In principle, they do not enjoy any autonomous right to move and reside freely. Core’ family members have an automatic right of entry and residence, irrespective of their nationality.
The following persons are defined in Article 2(2) of the Directive as ‘core’ family members:
- spouse
- registered partner
- cohabiting partner
- a child (or other direct descendant) under 21 or who is economically dependant
- parents (or other direct ascendants) who are economically dependant
- another family member who is dependent on an EEA citizen for his/her support, or is part of the EEA citizen’s household, or whom an EEA citizen is required to take care of personally, for serious health reasons.
Please note that EU citizens residing in the Member State of their own nationality do not normally benefit from the rights granted by the Directive (as there is no element of free movement), for example a Swedish citizen living in Sweden do not hold a right of residence based on Directive 2004/38/EC in his/her home country, unless he or she returned to Sweden after having utilised the right of free movement in another Member State for a certain period.
How to apply
You can apply for a short-stay Schengen visa (type C) which will allow you to enter and stay in Sweden and the Schengen area for up to 90 days.
Where family members decide not to make use of their right to lodge their application directly at the consulate but to use the services of an external service provider (VFS) or extra services, they may be required to pay for these services (service fee). Family members are exempted from paying the visa fee.
In the event that you wish to remain in the Member State for more than 90 days as a family member of a EU citizen exercising his/her free movement rights, you must apply (when in the Member State) for a Residence Card of a family member of an EU citizen.
Book an appointment
As a family member of an EU citizen, you also have the possibility to submit your visa application directly at the Swedish Embassy in Kenya.
Send an email to ambassaden.nairobivisum@gov.se (mailto:ambassaden.nairobivisum@gov.se) in order to receive an appointment. You will usually be given an appointment within a few working days.
More information
Directive 2004/38/EC (https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?qid=1487136010527&uri=CELEX%3A32004L0038)
The Swedish Migration Agency on EES family members (https://www.migrationsverket.se/English/Private-individuals/EU-EEA-citizens.html)
Third countries whose nationals or specific categories of such third country nationals who are subject to prior consultation 
Under Article 22 of the Visa Code, a Member State may require the central authorities of other Member States to consult its central authorities during the examination of visa applications lodged by nationals of specific third countries or specific categories of such nationals. Such consultation does not apply to applications for airport transit visas. The table below lists the third countries and the specific categories concerned. When a third country is listed it means that at least one Member State requires such prior consultation. The consultation might take 1-7 days.
Annexure 16 Prior Consultation List (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5cTmlz6WRVuHYfbRUygKDW/260f8052aad704ad0801fe40b53373b3/annex-16-prior-consultation-en.pdf)

## Visa Fees
New Visa Fees are effective from 27th January 2025
Please note that a family member of an EU or EEA citizen does NOT pay an application fee for Schengen visa.
There is a VFS service charge of Euro 9 (KES 1200) (payable via M-Pesa at the Visa application centre) levied per application.
Please enquire from the Visa Application centre for the payment procedure during application date.

## Documents Required
Get the peace of mind you need on your travels, with insurance made simple for more click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ken/en/swe/travel-insurance-non-schengen).
Please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1dfHkblufAvjkFXnAG3h3i/fe11f291566fc9bb201069e29d0f13d9/EU-EEA_family_members.pdf) to download the Checklist
To help us process your application quickly and accurately, please complete the Sweden Visa Application Form (VAF) electronically using the editable PDF available on this site, print the typed copy and carry it with appointment letter during your visit to the visa application centre. Completing the form electronically reduces counter waiting time and helps avoid errors caused by illegible handwriting.
Note: Applicants who arrive without a form completed electronically will still be accepted with a* completed handwritten form; however, they should expect additional processing time at Visa Application Centre*.
Sweden Visa Application Form - English (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5yHeDH1fk2QNWvEAdK4vVK/004680f021d9510e031c1fafd11ecf3c/Sweden-Visa-Application-Form-English.pdf)
Sweden Visa Application Form - Swedish (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7xr6u42ZqPERmjgcF08RPW/17d00aec8e7bbff236fb889498e1d395/Sweden-Visa-Application-Form-Swedish.pdf)
! Please be informed that it is imperative to provide documents in good condition and high-quality copies.
Copies of documents need to be done in Portrait mode
List of approved insurance companies (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/CHwwfuuEbQ2U5psvReoj9/3425dd85414ff7c95fd1b70383d9fccb/Updated-list-of-approved-insurance-companies-Medical-Insurance-Schengen-Region-2025.pdf)
Biometric data information for visa applicants (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2lG67ubwHR7fCszhDh0HLO/58f3d63c1e574033e683df71e83e6597/Biometric_Data_Information_for_Applicants-Feb-2026.pdf)
Family Members of EU citizens (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5pDeZfqiKZtaziz2QU94uT/7bad1b0629043a544f56b429c830f4dc/Family_Members_of_EU_Citizens-Feb-2026.pdf)
General information about visa application (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3kAdOVpNWnuAkm69CtzJPc/8dd0f125b85820280f9a8fe432731bd6/General_information_about_Visa_Application-Feb-2026.pdf)
How to apply for a visa (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/hfiLNjDIe3CWB4oTbKRYE/e53bb78bc09073745214d88ed8d1c361/How_to_Apply_for_a_Visa-Feb-2026.pdf)
Checklists (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2waML0olXwGKwlnT9FlJ3v/21e242f1babe53e5939f83bd2f784dd3/Checklist-for-Kenyan-Applicants-Feb-2026.pdf)

## Photo Specifications
Please provide one recent photograph (not more than 1 month old) of yourself, which should not have been used previously in the passport. The photograph should be in colour and: 
- Taken against a light background (white or off-white) so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear share quality and with the face in focus.
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print).
- Full face, non-smiling [without sunglasses, a hat/cap or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of their religious belief or ethnic background].
- Please stick the photograph on the Visa Application Form.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete. A photo booth
                                                                        meeting these requirements is available at the centre.
Kindly download the Photo Specification file (Pdf/photo-specification.pdf) for more information

## Processing Time
You need to submit your application in good time before travelling. According to article 9 section 1 in the visa code, EU 2019/1155, you should apply not less than 15 days before the planned visit begins. The 15 days is counted from when the application is received by the Embassy of Sweden in Nairobi. Consequently, you must calculate with the transportation time when planning for your submission.
A late application might be refused or in some cases a request for a new flight booking, insurance and even new invitation will be required. A request for new documents is likely to increase the handling time further.
The normal transportation time, one way is 1 working day for Nairobi, Kenya.
The Embassy should handle your application within 15 days (if the application is admissible), counted from when the Embassy has received the application. If an application is not complete or if an interview or document verification is needed the handling time can be extended up to 45 days.
General inquiries about Schengen visas should be addressed to 254 2051479 08 (tel:254 2051479 08) or info.Seke@vfshelpline.com (mailto:info.Seke@vfshelpline.com) . If you already have applied and have an inquiry please contact the Embassy of Sweden in Nairobi by email (ambassaden.nairobi-visum@gov.se (mailto:ambassaden.nairobi-visum@gov.se)) or phone. The Embassy´s phone hours are Monday and Wednesdays 2-3 pm and Fridays 11am-12noon local Kenyan time. See the Embassy´s Website for holidays and such at https://www.swedenabroad.se/en/embassies/kenya-nairobi/contact/ (https://www.swedenabroad.se/en/embassies/kenya-nairobi/contact/). During the peak season (May-July and November-December) you need to wait patiently for a reply (3-6 days).

## Download Forms

Consent Form For Children Under 18 (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/yxDQGSDuMP9VLnQHR9c2o/61f41dcd884fc8f0e966ad99d0633842/New-Consent-Form-For-Children-Under-18.pdf)
Schengen Visa Application Form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/KUfVCOfQ5sAGGMwhWe2ZZ/28f1f446f024a994ece522768c0bcea1/Sweden-New-Checklist.pdf)

Schengen Visa - EU/EEA family members Checklist 
Schengen Visa – Minors (under 18 years) (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3IsSVLGpJRtTmxxCK8T0HH/774d6a5b315999693eee9ea392f81874/KENYA-Minors-2024-07-23.pdf) 

To Download a Form: 

- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.

OR

- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

[Table:Business or conference]

Please Note:
The visa fee shall be waived for applicants belonging to one of the following categories:
- Children under six years
- School pupils, students, postgraduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake stays for the purpose of study or educational training
- Researchers travelling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research or participating in a scientific seminar or conference
- Representatives of non-profit organizations aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organized by non-profit organizations
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports when travelling for official duties
- Family members of EU/EEA citizens
- Family members of UK Nationals who fall under the EU-UK Withdrawal Agreement

- Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost. Please see more detail from Additional Services tab.

visa_types.processing_time:
You need to submit your application in good time before travelling. According to article 9 section 1 in the visa code, EU 2019/1155, you should apply not less than 15 days before the planned visit begins. The 15 days is counted from when the application is received by the Embassy of Sweden in Nairobi. Consequently, you must calculate with the transportation time when planning for your submission.
A late application might be refused or in some cases a request for a new flight booking, insurance and even new invitation will be required. A request for new documents is likely to increase the handling time further.
The normal transportation time, one way is 1 working day for Nairobi, Kenya.
The Embassy should handle your application within 15 days (if the application is admissible), counted from when the Embassy has received the application. If an application is not complete or if an interview or document verification is needed the handling time can be extended up to 45 days.
General inquiries about Schengen visas should be addressed to 254 2051479 08 (tel:254 2051479 08) or info.Seke@vfshelpline.com (mailto:info.Seke@vfshelpline.com) . If you already have applied and have an inquiry please contact the Embassy of Sweden in Nairobi by email (ambassaden.nairobi-visum@gov.se (mailto:ambassaden.nairobi-visum@gov.se)) or phone. The Embassy´s phone hours are Monday and Wednesdays 2-3 pm and Fridays 11am-12noon local Kenyan time. See the Embassy´s Website for holidays and such at https://www.swedenabroad.se/en/embassies/kenya-nairobi/contact/ (https://www.swedenabroad.se/en/embassies/kenya-nairobi/contact/). During the peak season (May-July and November-December) you need to wait patiently for a reply (3-6 days).
visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you wish to travel to Sweden to receive medical treatment, please see the list of requirements for further details. Please note that you will need medical documentation from a treating
                                                                        doctor in your home country as well as a contact in Sweden. The cost of the treatment and the reason why it cannot be performed in your home country must also be documented.
Third countries whose nationals or specific categories of such third country nationals who are subject to prior consultation 
Under Article 22 of the Visa Code, a Member State may require the central authorities of other Member States to consult its central authorities during the examination of visa applications lodged by nationals of specific third countries or specific categories of such nationals. Such consultation does not apply to applications for airport transit visas. The table below lists the third countries and the specific categories concerned. When a third country is listed it means that at least one Member State requires such prior consultation. The consultation might take 1-7 days.
Annexure 16 Prior Consultation List (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5cTmlz6WRVuHYfbRUygKDW/260f8052aad704ad0801fe40b53373b3/annex-16-prior-consultation-en.pdf)

Please provide one recent photograph (not more than 1 month old) of yourself, which should not have been used previously in the passport. The photograph should be in colour and: 
- Taken against a light background (white or off-white) so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear share quality and with the face in focus.
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print).
- Full face, non-smiling [without sunglasses, a hat/cap or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of their religious belief or ethnic background].
- Please stick the photograph on the Visa Application Form.

## Photo Specifications
Please provide one recent photograph (not more than 1 month old) of yourself, which should not have been used previously in the passport. The photograph should be in colour and:
- Taken against a light background (white or off-white) so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear share quality and with the face in focus.
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print).
- Full face, non-smiling [without sunglasses, a hat/cap or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of their religious belief or ethnic background].
- Please stick the photograph on the Visa Application Form.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete. A photo booth
                                                                        meeting these requirements is available at the centre.
Kindly download the Photo Specification file (Pdf/photo-specification.pdf) for more information

## Processing Time
You need to submit your application in good time before travelling. According to article 9 section 1 in the visa code, EU 2019/1155, you should apply not less than 15 days before the planned visit begins. The 15 days is counted from when the application is received by the Embassy of Sweden in Nairobi. Consequently, you must calculate with the transportation time when planning for your submission.
A late application might be refused or in some cases a request for a new flight booking, insurance and even new invitation will be required. A request for new documents is likely to increase the handling time further.
The normal transportation time, one way is 1 working day for Nairobi, Kenya.
The Embassy should handle your application within 15 days (if the application is admissible), counted from when the Embassy has received the application. If an application is not complete or if an interview or document verification is needed the handling time can be extended up to 45 days.
General inquiries about Schengen visas should be addressed to 254 2051479 08 (tel:254 2051479 08) or info.Seke@vfshelpline.com (mailto:info.Seke@vfshelpline.com) . If you already have applied and have an inquiry please contact the Embassy of Sweden in Nairobi by email (ambassaden.nairobi-visum@gov.se (mailto:ambassaden.nairobi-visum@gov.se)) or phone. The Embassy´s phone hours are Monday and Wednesdays 2-3 pm and Fridays 11am-12noon local Kenyan time. See the Embassy´s Website for holidays and such at https://www.swedenabroad.se/en/embassies/kenya-nairobi/contact/ (https://www.swedenabroad.se/en/embassies/kenya-nairobi/contact/). During the peak season (May-July and November-December) you need to wait patiently for a reply (3-6 days).

Overview
If you wish to travel to Sweden as a tourist, please follow these instructions. An invitation is not necessary, but you will have to submit documents related to your financial situation
                                                                        and establishment in Kenya as well as a detailed travel itinerary etc.
Third countries whose nationals or specific categories of such third country nationals who are subject to prior consultation 
Under Article 22 of the Visa Code, a Member State may require the central authorities of other Member States to consult its central authorities during the examination of visa applications lodged by nationals of specific third countries or specific categories of such nationals. Such consultation does not apply to applications for airport transit visas. The table below lists the third countries and the specific categories concerned. When a third country is listed it means that at least one Member State requires such prior consultation. The consultation might take 1-7 days.

Note: Applicants who arrive without a form completed electronically will still be accepted with a* completed handwritten form; however, they should expect additional processing time at Visa Application Centre*.

## Photo Specifications
Please provide one recent photograph (not more than 1 month old) of yourself, which should not have been used previously in the passport. The photograph should be in colour and: 
- Taken against a light background (white or off-white) so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear share quality and with the face in focus.
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print).
- Full face, non-smiling [without sunglasses, a hat/cap or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of their religious belief or ethnic background].
- Please stick the photograph on the Visa Application Form.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete. A photo booth
                                                                        meeting these requirements is available at the centre.
Kindly download the Photo Specification file (Pdf/photo-specification.pdf) for more information

## Processing Time
You need to submit your application in good time before travelling. According to article 9 section 1 in the visa code, EU 2019/1155, you should apply not less than 15 days before the planned visit begins. The 15 days is counted from when the application is received by the Embassy of Sweden in Nairobi. Consequently, you must calculate with the transportation time when planning for your submission.
A late application might be refused or in some cases a request for a new flight booking, insurance and even new invitation will be required. A request for new documents is likely to increase the handling time further.
The normal transportation time, one way is 1 working day for Nairobi, Kenya.
The Embassy should handle your application within 15 days (if the application is admissible), counted from when the Embassy has received the application. If an application is not complete or if an interview or document verification is needed the handling time can be extended up to 45 days.
General inquiries about Schengen visas should be addressed to 254 2051479 08 (tel:254 2051479 08) or info.Seke@vfshelpline.com (mailto:info.Seke@vfshelpline.com) . If you already have applied and have an inquiry please contact the Embassy of Sweden in Nairobi by email (ambassaden.nairobi-visum@gov.se (mailto:ambassaden.nairobi-visum@gov.se)) or phone. The Embassy´s phone hours are Monday and Wednesdays 2-3 pm and Fridays 11am-12noon local Kenyan time. See the Embassy´s Website for holidays and such at https://www.swedenabroad.se/en/embassies/kenya-nairobi/contact/ (https://www.swedenabroad.se/en/embassies/kenya-nairobi/contact/). During the peak season (May-July and November-December) you need to wait patiently for a reply (3-6 days).
